response: The problem requires us to find the multiplicative inverse of 450 modulo 3599. This means we need to find an integer \( n \) such that \( 450n \equiv 1 \pmod{3599} \). Since 450 and 3599 are coprime, the multiplicative inverse exists and can be found using the Extended Euclidean Algorithm. The steps are as follows: 1. Use the Euclidean Algorithm to find the greatest common divisor (GCD) of 450 and 3599. 2. Use the Extended Euclidean Algorithm to express the GCD as a linear combination of 450 and 3599. Since the GCD is 1, we can find coefficients \( x \) and \( y \) such that \( 450x + 3599y = 1 \). The coefficient \( x \) will be the multiplicative inverse of 450 modulo 3599. 3. By running the Extended Euclidean Algorithm, we get: \( 3599 = 8 \cdot 450 + 199 \) \( 450 = 2 \cdot 199 + 52 \) \( 199 = 3 \cdot 52 + 43 \) \( 52 = 1 \cdot 43 + 9 \) \( 43 = 4 \cdot 9 + 7 \) \( 9 = 1 \cdot 7 + 2 \) \( 7 = 3 \cdot 2 + 1 \) \( 2 = 2 \cdot 1 + 0 \) The GCD is 1, so the multiplicative inverse exists. Back-substituting, we get: \( 1 = 7 - 3 \cdot 2 = 7 - 3 \cdot (9 - 1 \cdot 7) = 4 \cdot 7 - 3 \cdot 9 = 4 \cdot (43 - 4 \cdot 9) - 3 \cdot 9 = 4 \cdot 43 - 19 \cdot 9 = 4 \cdot 43 - 19 \cdot (52 - 1 \cdot 43) = 23 \cdot 43 - 19 \cdot 52 = 23 \cdot (199 - 3 \cdot 52) - 19 \cdot 52 = 23 \cdot 199 - 86 \cdot 52 = 23 \cdot 199 - 86 \cdot (450 - 2 \cdot 199) = 195 \cdot 199 - 86 \cdot 450 = 195 \cdot (3599 - 8 \cdot 450) - 86 \cdot 450 = 195 \cdot 3599 - 1670 \cdot 450 \) So, the multiplicative inverse of 450 modulo 3599 is \(-1670\), which is equivalent to \(3599 - 1670 = 1929\). Therefore, the multiplicative inverse of 450 modulo 3599 is \(1929\). </think> <answer>1929</answer>
